Since the portfolios are already in increasing order, the first quartile (Q.) is given by the Sharpe ratio of Portfolio 2 and the third quartile (Q₂) by the Sharpe ratio of Portfolio 6. Hence, IQR = Q3- Q1 = 1.4-0.6 =0.8. A is incorrect because it computes the wrong locations for the first and third quartiles. The location of the first quartile (Q,) and the third quartile (Q₁) with a sample of size 7 should be calculated as: (7+1)(25/100) = 2 and (7 + 1) (75/100)=6, respectively. However, if the "+1" is omitted, the locations will be incorrectly assumed to be 7(25/100) = 1.75 and 7(75/100)=5.25. Since these locations are not whole numbers, we must use linear interpolation. The difference between the Sharpe ratio of Portfolio 2 and Portfolio 1 is 0.5-0.1=0.4, hence the interpolated value at position 1.75 is 0.2+ (0.75)(0.4) = 0.2+0.3 = 0.5. Similarly, the interpolated value at position 5.25 would be 1.0+ (0.25)(1.4-1.0) = 1.0+0.1 = 1.1. Hence, IQR= Q3-Q1 = 1.1-0.5=0.6. C is incorrect because it is the range, rather than the interquartile range, of the Sharpe ratios. The range is the difference between the maximum and minimum values in a dataset. Hence, the range of the Sharpe ratios is 1.6- 0.2 = 1.4.

questions.question: An analyst gathers the following information about a company: Before-tax cost of debt 5% Marginal tax rate 30% Cost of equity 8% Target debt-to-equity ratio 50% The company's WACC is equal to:
questions.explanation: B is correct. WACC = wdrd (1-t) + wprp+were. Given that there is no preferred stock in the capital structure, WACC= wdrd (1-1)+were Also, a simple way of transforming a debt-to-equity ratio D/E into a weight that is, D/(D + E) is to divide D/E by 1 + D/E As a result, WACC = (D/E)/[1+ (D/E)]×rd×(1-t)+1/(1+ (D/E)xre] = 0.5/(1+0.5) x 0.05 x (1 - 0.30) + 1/(1+0.5) x 0.08 = 6.50%. A is incorrect because this distractor incorrectly interprets the debt-to-equity ratio as 50% weight for debt and 50% weight for equity. WACC # wdrd (1-t) + wprp+were= 0.5× 0.05×(1-0.30)+0.5× 0.08 = 5.75%. C is incorrect because this distractor incorrectly omits the tax rate. WACC # (D/E)/[1 + (D/E)x rd]+1/(1+ (D/E)x re] = 0.5/(1+0.5) x 0.05 + 1/(1+0.5) x 0.08 = 7.00%.

questions.question: Based on good corporate governance practices, it is most appropriate for a company's compensation committee to:

options.C: recommend remuneration for the external auditors.

questions.explanation: B is correct. Under good corporate governance practices the compensation committee develops remuneration policies for directors as well as key executives. A is incorrect because the corporate governance committee should be composed of independent (non-executive) members only. C is incorrect because the audit committee is responsible for proposing the external auditor's remuneration.

questions.question: If differences between accounting profit and taxable income are recoverable, deferred tax assets are created when:

options.A: accounting profit is less than taxable income.
options.B: accounting profit is greater than taxable income.
options.C: income taxes payable is less than income tax expense.

questions.explanation: A is correct. Deferred tax assets arise when an excess amount is paid for income taxes (taxable income higher than accounting profit) and the company expects to recover the difference during the course of future operations. B is incorrect because it has the relationship reversed. Deferred tax assets arise when an excess amount is paid for income taxes (taxable income higher than accounting profit) and the company expects to recover the difference during the course of future operations. C is incorrect because it has the relationship reversed; when income taxes payable exceed income tax expense, that creates a deferred tax asset. Deferred tax assets arise when an excess amount is paid for income taxes (taxable income higher than accounting profit) and the company expects to recover the difference during the course of future operations.

questions.question: An analyst gathers the following exchange rate information: Spot rate 4.6895 1-year forward points -12.7 The 1-year forward rate is closest to:
questions.explanation: B is correct. To convert any of these quoted forward points into a forward rate, one would divide the number of points by 10,000 (to scale down to the fourth decimal place, the last decimal place in the spot quote) and then add the result to the spot exchange rate quote 4.6895+(-12.7/10,000)=4.6895 - 0.00127 = 4.68823. A is incorrect because the forward points are scaled down by 1,000 instead of 10,000: 4.6895+(- 12.7/1.000) = 4.6895 -0.0127 = 4.67680. C is incorrect because the forward points are subtracted from the spot rate: 4.6895 - (-12.7/10,000) = 4.6895 + 0.00127 = 4.69077.

questions.question: An investor plans to contribute $10,000 each year into an account that earns an annual interest rate of 5%. If the first contribution is made one year from today, the value of the account immediately after the 10th contribution is closest to:
questions.explanation: A is correct. It is the future value of the ordinary annuity: FV = A [(1+r)N-1]/r=$10,000[(1+0.05)10 - 1)/0.05 = $125,779. Calculator solution: [PMT] 10,000; [N] = 10; [I/Y] = 5; [CPT] [FV] = 125,779. B is incorrect because it is the future value of an annuity due (not an ordinary annuity): FV of annuity due = (1+r) FV of ordinary annuity = (1+0.05) x $125,779 = $132,068. Compared to an ordinary annuity, the payments in an annuity due are each discounted one less period. Therefore, we can modify [the formula for the FV of an ordinary annuity] to handle annuities due by multiplying the right-hand side of the equation by (1+r). Calculator solution: in BGN mode; [PMT] = 10,000: [N] = 10; [I/Y] = 5; [CPT] [FV] 132,068. C is incorrect because it assumes the investment is an 11-year (instead of a 10-year) ordinary annuity, thus calculating FV = $10,000[(1+0.05)11-1]/0.05= $142,068. Calculator solution: [PMT] = 10,000; [N] = 11: [I/Y] = 5; [CPT] [FV] = 142,068.

questions.question: According to Modigliani and Miller's Proposition II without taxes, a higher proportion of debt in the capital structure of a company results in:

options.C: a greater company value.

questions.explanation: A is correct. The more debt the company uses, the greater the cost of equity, but the total value of the company does not change and neither does the weighted average cost of capital. B is incorrect because the cost of equity increases. The more debt the company uses, the greater the cost of equity, but the total value of the company does not change and neither does the weighted average cost of capital. C is incorrect because the value of the company remains the same. The more debt the company uses, the greater the cost of equity, but the total value of the company does not change and neither does the weighted average cost of capital.

questions.question: In a small country, an export subsidy for a good most likely results in a decrease in the good's domestic:

options.C: consumption.

questions.explanation: C is correct. As the price increases, domestic consumption will most likely decrease. A is incorrect because an export subsidy raises the price in the domestic market by the amount of the subsidy (price before subsidy plus subsidy). B is incorrect because in an export subsidy, there will be an increase in domestic production.

questions.question: In the statement of cash flows, interest paid by a company is most likely included in:

options.A: either the operating or financing section under IFRS.
options.B: either the operating or financing section under US GAAP.
options.C: only the financing section under both IFRS and US GAAP.

questions.explanation: A is correct. US GAAP requires that interest paid be classified as an operating cash flow; IFRS allows interest paid to be classified as either an operating or financing activity. B is incorrect because US GAAP requires that interest paid be classified as an operating cash flow; IFRS allows interest paid to be classified as either an operating or financing activity. C is incorrect because US GAAP requires that interest paid be classified as an operating cash flow; IFRS allows interest paid to be classified as either an operating or financing activity.

questions.question: An analyst is comparing the solvency of a company over the past two years using the information below: Year 2 * Millions Total debt 2,300 Total shareholders' equity 17,000 Total assets 20,000 Net income 375 Interest payments/interest expense 200 Taxes paid 125 Ratios in Year 1 Debt to capital 12.7% Interest coverage 2.9 The best conclusion the analyst can make about Year 2 is that compared with Year 1, the company's solvency has:

options.B: deteriorated because both ratios have weakened.
options.C: improved because both ratios have strengthened.

questions.explanation: C is correct. Both ratios have improved from Year 1 to Year 2, thus the company is more solvent in Year 2. Year 1 Year 2 calculations Year 2 Debt to capital 12.7% 2,300/(2,300+ 17,000) = 11.9% 11.9% Interest coverage 2.9 (375+200+125)/200 = 3.5 3.5. A is incorrect because both ratios have improved from Year 1 to Year 2, thus the company is more solvent in Year 2. B is incorrect because it calculates interest coverage using net income instead of EBIT (1.875), and interprets the change in debt to capital as a deterioration because the value has decreased.

questions.question: Which action is most likely considered a secondary source of liquidity?

options.A: Increasing the availability of bank lines of credit
options.B: Increasing the efficiency of cash flow management
options.C: Renegotiating current debt contracts to lower interest payments

questions.explanation: C is correct. Renegotiating debt contracts is a secondary source of liquidity because it may affect the company's operating and/or financial positions. A is incorrect because increasing bank lines of credit is a primary source of liquidity. B is incorrect because increasing cash flow management efficiency is a primary source of liquidity.

questions.question: The returns of a fund are as follows: Year Return (%) 1 -20.60 2 15.00 3 0.50 4 9.80 5 4.60 The mean absolute deviation (MAD) of returns for the fund is closest to:
questions.explanation: A is correct. The mean absolute deviation (MAD) for a sample is calculated as follows: MAD = (sum of |Xi - X-bar| for i = 1 to n) / n, where Xi = the return of the fund during year i X-bar = the mean of the returns of the sample n=the number of returns in the sample i=the index for the year In this problem: Mean: X= (-20.60% +15.00% +0.50% 9.80% +4.60%)/5 = 1.86% Deviations from Mean Absolute Deviation (%) |−20.60% −1.86% | 22.46 |15.00% − 1.86%| 13.14 |10.50% −1.86%| 1.36 |19.80% −1.86%| 7.94 |4.60% − 1.86%| 2.74 Total 47.64 MAD = 47.64/5 9.53.

questions.question: The time-weighted rate of return:

options.A: is affected by the amount and timing of cash flows to and from a portfolio.
options.B: calculates multi-period cash flows mirroring a portfolio's compound growth rate.
options.C: results in a lower return when compared with the money-weighted rate of return.

questions.explanation: B is correct. Time-weighted rate of return reflects the compound rate of growth of one unit of currency invested over a stated measurement period, and it removes the effects of timing and amount of withdrawals and additions to the portfolio. A is incorrect because this is the explanation for money-weighted return. C is incorrect because time-weighted rate of return can be the same, higher, or lower than money-weighted rate of return.

questions.question: An analyst gathers the following information about a company: Year 2 Year 1 Revenue € 725,000 € 720,000 EBIT € 93,500 € 91,500 Net income € 57,500 € 58,000 Weighted average common shares outstanding during the year 650,000 635,000 Which of the following increased from Year 1 to Year 2?

options.C: Net profit margin

questions.explanation: B is correct. EBIT margin = EBIT / Total revenues. Year 1 =€91,500/€720,000 ~= 12.71% Year 2 =€93,500/€725,000 ~= 12.90% Accordingly, EBIT margin increased from Year 1 to Year 2. A is incorrect because Basic EPS = Net income/Weighted average number of ordinary shares outstanding. Year 1= €58,000/635,000 ~= 0.0913 Year 2= €57,500/650,000 ~= 0.0885 Accordingly, Basic EPS decreased from Year 1 to Year 2. C is incorrect because Net profit margin = Net income / Total revenues. Year 1= €58,000/€720,000 ~= 8.06% Year 2= €57,500/€725,000 ~= 7.93% Accordingly, Net profit margin decreased from Year 1 to Year 2.

questions.question: All else being equal, in an environment of rising inventory unit costs and constant inventory quantities, the LIFO inventory valuation method most likely results in a higher:

options.A: ending inventory than under the FIFO method.
options.B: gross profit margin than under the FIFO method.
options.C: inventory turnover ratio than under the FIFO method.

questions.explanation: C is correct. In an environment of rising inventory unit costs and constant or increasing inventory quantities, FIFO (in comparison with weighted average cost or LIFO) will allocate a lower amount of the total cost of goods available for sale to cost of sales on the income statement and a higher amount to ending inventory on the balance sheet. Consequently, under LIFO the cost of goods sold is higher and inventory is lower, so inventory turnover (Cost of sales / Average inventory) is higher than under FIFO. A is incorrect because in an environment of rising inventory unit costs and constant or increasing inventory quantities, FIFO (in comparison with weighted average cost or LIFO) will allocate a lower amount of the total cost of goods available for sale to cost of sales on the income statement and a higher amount to ending inventory on the balance sheet. B is incorrect because in an environment of rising inventory unit costs and constant or increasing inventory quantities, FIFO (in comparison with weighted average cost or LIFO) will allocate a lower amount of the total cost of goods available for sale to cost of sales on the income statement and a higher amount to ending inventory on the balance sheet. Consequently, under LIFO the cost of sales is higher than under FIFO, which leads to a lower, not higher, gross profit margin (Gross profit/Revenue) than under FIFO.

questions.question: For a company reporting under IFRS, which of the following events most likely represents low financial reporting quality? The company:

options.A: reported an increase in EPS as a result of the sale of a subsidiary.
options.B: included gains from foreign exchange rate changes in its cost of goods sold.
options.C: entered a long-term lease for a customized piece of equipment and classified it as a finance lease.

questions.explanation: B is correct. High financial reporting quality provides useful information to decision makers. Since foreign exchange gains and losses may not recur, they should be disclosed separately and not included in cost of goods sold. A is incorrect because if properly disclosed, an increase in EPS from the sale of a subsidiary does not represent low quality financial reporting, but it may be low quality earnings. C is incorrect because long-term leases for customized pieces of equipment should be reported as finance leases and conforms to IFRS, therefore this is not low quality reporting.

questions.question: An analyst gathers the following information (in $ millions) about a company reporting under US GAAP: Cash flow from operations 500 Capital expenditures 40 Interest expensed and paid 20 Net borrowing 10 If the tax rate is 25%, free cash flow to the firm (in $ millions) is:
questions.explanation: C is correct. FCFF = CFO + Int(1-Tax rate) - FCInv. Free cash flow to the firm is calculated as $500 + $20x(1-0.25) - $40=$500+$15-$40 = $475. A is incorrect because FCFF = CFO + Int(1-Tax rate) - FCInv. The answer choice $460 ignores the net-of-tax interest and is calculated as $500 - $40. B is incorrect because it calculates FCFE. The value of $470 million is calculated as $500-$40 + $10, which represents free cash flow to equity. FCFE= CFO – FCInv + Net borrowing.

questions.question: For a hypothesis test concerning the correlation between two normally distributed variables with sample size n each, the number of degrees of freedom is:
questions.explanation: A is correct. If the two variables are normally distributed, we can test to determine whether the null hypothesis (H0: p = 0) should be rejected. This test statistic is t-distributed with n-2 degrees of freedom, where p represents the population correlation coefficient. B is incorrect because if the two variables are normally distributed, we can test to determine whether the null hypothesis (Ho: p = 0) should be rejected. This test statistic is f-distributed with n-2 degrees of freedom. C is incorrect because if the two variables are normally distributed, we can test to determine whether the null hypothesis (H0: p = 0) should be rejected. This test statistic is t-distributed with n-2 degrees of freedom.

questions.question: A portfolio has two stocks with equal weighting. The variance of returns for each stock is 100 percent squared, and the covariance is 50 percent squared. The portfolio standard deviation of returns is closest to:
questions.explanation: B is correct. 𝜎² (Rp) = ∑ ∑ 𝑤𝑖𝑤𝑗 𝑛 𝑗=1 𝑛 𝑗=1 𝐶𝑜𝑣(𝑅𝑖, 𝑅𝑗) For two assets, n = 2, 𝜎² (Rp) = w12𝜎21+w22𝜎22+2w1w2𝜎21,2 where, from given values, σ² = 𝜎² = 100%2, 𝜎1,22= 50%.2 Since the portfolio is equally weighted, w = 1/n = ½ Hence, the portfolio variance𝜎 ²(Rp) = (0.5)2 (100) + (0.5)2 (100)+2(0.5)(0.5)(50) =1/4(100+100+100) = 300/4=75 [%2], i.e. portfolio standard deviation, 𝜎 (Rp) = Sqr(0.0075) = 0.08660~0.087 = 8.7%. A is incorrect because it is the portfolio standard deviation calculated from an incorrect formula for portfolio variance. where the "2" is omitted from the covariance term, i.e. 𝜎 2(Rp) = w12𝜎21+w22𝜎22+w₁w2𝜎1,22 = (%) (100) + (0.5)2 (100)+(0.5)2(100)+(0.5)(0.5)(50)=0.25(100+100+50) 250/4 = 62.5 [%]2. which gives a portfolio standard deviation of 𝜎 (Rp) = Sqr(0.00625) = 0.07906 ~0.079 = 7.9%. C is incorrect because it is the portfolio variance (Rp) = (0.5)2(100) + (0.5)(100)+2(0.5)(0.5)(50) = 75 [%2], incorrectly expressed in %, and not the portfolio standard deviation.

questions.question: A researcher wants to measure the level of consumer confidence in a country by interviewing a simple random sample of ten consumers from three randomly selected cities. This method is an example of:

options.C: stratified random sampling.

questions.explanation: A is correct. Cluster sampling requires the division or classification of the population into subpopulation groups, called clusters. In this method, the population is divided into clusters, each of which is essentially a mini-representation of the entire populations. Then certain clusters are chosen as a whole using simple random sampling. If a subsample is randomly selected from each selected cluster, then the plan is referred as two-stage cluster sampling. In this case, each city represents a cluster, from which a sample of ten consumers is randomly selected, i.e. the researcher uses a two-stage cluster sampling. B is incorrect because with systematic sampling, we select every kth member until we have a sample of the desired size. Systematic sampling does not involve the division or classification of the population into subpopulation groups. C is incorrect because a major difference between cluster and stratified random samples is that in cluster sampling, a whole cluster is regarded as a sampling unit and only sampled clusters are included. In stratified random sampling, however, all the strata are included and only specific elements within each stratum are then selected as sampling unit. Also, in stratified random sampling, the population is divided into subpopulations (strata) based on one or more classification criteria. Simple random samples are then drawn from each stratum in sizes proportional to the relative size of each stratum in the population. This would have been a stratified random sampling if the researcher randomly selected consumers from each city based on the size of each city relative to the whole population.

questions.question: The following data apply to a firm operating in perfect competition. Quantity Total Revenue Total Cost 21 $210 $138 22 $220 $145 23 $230 $154 24 $240 $165 The firm's profit maximizing output (in units) is most likely:
questions.explanation: B is correct. Under perfect competition, economic profits are maximized when marginal revenue equals short-run marginal cost-in this case, marginal cost crosses $10 per unit. Profits are maximized at 23 units of production because marginal cost is in excess of marginal revenue at 24 units. A is incorrect because higher quantities deliver marginal revenue above marginal cost. C is incorrect because at 24 units, marginal costs exceed marginal revenue. The Firm and Market Structures determine and interpret breakeven and shutdown points of production, as well as how economies and diseconomies of scale affect costs under perfect and imperfect competition.

questions.question: The money supply most likely increases when a central bank:

options.C: raises the reserve requirement.

questions.explanation: A is correct. If the central bank wishes to increase the supply of money, it might buy bonds (usually government bonds) from the banks, with an agreement to sell them back at some time in the future. This transaction is known as a repurchase agreement. B is incorrect because through the setting of a policy rate, a central bank can manipulate the amount of money in the money markets. Generally speaking, the higher the policy rate, the higher the potential penalty that banks will have to pay to the central bank if they run short of liquidity, the greater their willingness to reduce lending, and the more likely that broad money growth will shrink. C is incorrect because a central bank could restrict money creation by raising the reserve requirements of banks.

questions.question: Under IFRS, which component of the change in the net pension asset or liability each period is recognized in other comprehensive income?

options.B: Actuarial gains and losses and other remeasurements
options.C: The net interest expense or income accrued on the beginning net pension plan asset or liability

questions.explanation: B is correct. Under IFRS, the change in the net pension asset or liability each period is viewed as having three general components. Two of the components of this change are recognized as pension expense in profit and loss: (1) employees' service costs and (2) the net interest expense or income accrued on the beginning net pension asset or liability. The third item, remeasurements, is recognized in other comprehensive income. A is incorrect because employees' service costs are reported as pension expense in profit and loss. C is incorrect because the net interest expense or income accrued on the beginning net pension plan asset or liability is reported as pension expense in profit and loss.

questions.question: The following information is available for a company: Year 2 (in € Millions) EBIT (earnings before interest and taxes) 1,015.0 Interest expense 73.4 Tax expense 201.4 Total assets 5,305.0 Average total assets 5,421.0 Total debt 1,048.0 Year 1 Interest coverage 15.3x Debt to total assets 18.2% Operating return on assets (ROA) 17.3% Compared with Year 1, which of the following ratios most likely indicates an improvement in the creditworthiness of the company? The change in the company's:

options.C: debt-to-total assets.

questions.explanation: A is correct. When calculated for Year 2, interest coverage decreased and debt to total assets and return on assets both increased. In general, a decrease in interest coverage and an increase in debt to total assets would reduce creditworthiness, but an increase in return on assets would improve creditworthiness. Calculations are as follows: Calculation Year2 Comment Debt to total assets Total debt/Total assets = 19,8% Higher than year 1 (18.2%), €1,048/€5,305 =19.8% which would reduce creditworthiness Interest coverage EBIT/Interest payments 13,8x Lower than Year 1(15.3x), = €1,015/€73.4 which would reduce creditworthiness Operating ROA EBIT/Average total assets = 18,7% Higher than year 1 (17.3%), €1,015/€5,421 = which would improve creditworthiness. B is incorrect because a decrease in the interest coverage ratio would likely reduce creditworthiness. C is incorrect because an increase in debt-to-total assets would likely reduce creditworthiness.

questions.question: When considering ESG factors, energy efficiency is best described as a(n):

options.C: governance factor.

questions.explanation: A is correct. Energy efficiency is best described as an environmental factor. Environmental factors that are generally considered material in investment analysis include natural resource management, pollution prevention, water conservation, energy efficiency and reduced emissions, the existence of carbon assets, and adherence to environmental safety and regulatory standards. B is incorrect because energy efficiency is best described as an environmental factor, not a social factor. C is incorrect because energy efficiency is best described as an environmental factor, not a governance factor.

questions.question: An analyst gathers the following information about a company: Target D/E 0.80 Current D/E 0.65 Yield to maturity 4% Cost of equity 9% Tax rate 30% The WACC to be used in financial analysis is closest to:
questions.explanation: A is correct. The target D/E ratio is used to determine WACC. The target capital structure is the company's chosen proportions of debt and equity as opposed to the actual weightings. If we know the company's target capital structure, then of course we should use this in our analysis. Weight of debt, wd = (D/E)/(1+(D/E)) = 0.8/(1+0.8) = 0.4444 Weight of equity, we= 1-w=0.5556 WACC= wdrd (1-t)+wprp+were=(0.4444) (4%)(1-30%)+0+(0.5556)(9%) = 6.2444% ~6.24% where wd = the target proportion of debt in the capital structure when the company raises new funds rd =the before-tax marginal cost of debt t = the company's marginal tax rate wp =the target proportion of preferred stock in the capital structure when the company raises new funds rp = the marginal cost of preferred stock wp =the target proportion of common stock in the capital structure when the company raises new funds re =the marginal cost of common stock. B is incorrect because the current capital structure (determined by using the current D/E ratio) was used instead of the target capital structure. The target capital structure is the company's chosen proportions of debt and equity as opposed to the actual weightings. If we know the company's target capital structure, then of course we should use this in our analysis. Weight of equity, we = (D/E)/(1+(D/E)) = 0.65/(1+0.65) = 0.3939.

questions.question: In the event that the value of inventory declines below the carrying amount on the balance sheet, the inventory carrying amount must be written down and the reduction in value recognized as a(n):

options.B: decrease in the revaluation surplus account.
options.C: decrease in the inventory valuation allowance account.

questions.explanation: A is correct. In the event that the value of inventory declines below the carrying amount on the balance sheet, the inventory carrying amount must be written down to its net realizable value and the loss (reduction in value) recognized as an expense on the income statement. B is incorrect because in the event that the value of inventory declines below the carrying amount on the balance sheet, the inventory carrying amount must be written down to its net realizable value and the loss (reduction in value) recognized as an expense on the income statement. Also, the revaluation surplus account is used under the revaluation model for long-lived assets (and not inventory). Thus, IFRS allow companies to value long-lived assets either under a cost model at historical cost minus accumulated depreciation or amortization or under a revaluation model at fair value if a revaluation initially increases the carrying amount of the asset class, the increase in the carrying amount of the asset class bypasses the income statement and goes directly to equity under the heading of revaluation surplus. Any subsequent decrease in the asset's value first decreases the revaluation surplus and then goes to income. C is incorrect because in the event that the value of inventory declines below the carrying amount on the balance sheet. The inventory carrying amount must be written down to its net realizable value and the loss (reduction in value) recognized as an expense on the income statement. Also, in case an inventory valuation allowance is used, its balance should increase (and not decrease) because frequently, rather than writing inventory down directly, an inventory valuation allowance account is used. The allowance account is netted with the inventory accounts to arrive at the carrying amount that appears on the balance sheet.

questions.question: The international organization most likely to provide funds to create basic economic infrastructure in developing countries is the:

options.C: International Monetary Fund.

questions.explanation: A is correct. The World Bank helps to create the basic economic infrastructure that is essential for the creation of domestic financial markets and a well-functioning financial industry in developing countries. B is incorrect because the WTO's (World Trade Organization's) most important functions are the implementation, administration, and operation of individual agreements; acting as a platform for negotiations; and settling disputes. The WTO's primary focus is the promotion of international free trade, not creating basic economic infrastructure. C is incorrect because the focus of the International Monetary Fund (IMF) is more short term, such as supporting exchange-rate flexibility and lending money on a temporary basis. Basic economic infrastructure issues are not the IMF's focus.

questions.question: If a paired comparison test of mean differences supports rejecting the null hypothesis, then the:

options.A: difference in means is not statistically significant.
options.B: independence of the samples is statistically significant.
options.C: standard error of the mean differences is low relative to the sample mean difference.

questions.explanation: C is correct. According to the test statistic, 𝑡= 𝑑̅−𝑢0 𝑠𝑑̅ the lower the standard error in the denominator, the higher the value of the f-statistic. The t-statistic calculation includes the sample mean difference in the numerator. Therefore, a lower standard error (denominator) relative to the sample mean difference (numerator) results in a higher f-statistic value. A is incorrect because if the null hypothesis is rejected, the evidence is statistically significant. B is incorrect because when we want to conduct tests on two means based on samples that we believe are dependent. we use the test of the mean of the differences (a paired comparisons test). Therefore, it is not used to test whether samples are dependent or independent.

questions.question: The possibility of new entrants into an industry is considered in the calculation of:

options.C: neither the concentration ratio nor the Herfindahl-Hirschman index.

questions.explanation: C is correct. Just like the concentration ratio, the HHI does not take the possibility of entry into account. A is incorrect because just like the concentration ratio, the HHI does not take the possibility of entry into account. B is incorrect because just like the concentration ratio, the HHI does not take the possibility of entry into account.

questions.question: Under the indirect method, which of the following should be subtracted from net income to arrive at cash flow from operating activities?

options.C: Amortization expense of intangible assets

questions.explanation: B is correct. A decrease in current operating liabilities (e.g., accounts payable and accrued expense liabilities) is a subtraction from net income using the indirect method. A is incorrect because the purchase of equipment is a cash flow from investing activities, not an operating cash flow. Investing activities include purchasing and selling long-term assets and other investments. These long-term assets and other investments include property, plant, and equipment. C is incorrect because amortization expense of intangible assets is an addition to net income using the indirect method.

questions.question: An analyst gathers the following probabilities concerning Event X and some new information: Prior probability of X 0.70 Probability of the new information given X 0.50 Unconditional probability of the new information 0.60 Using Bayes' formula, the updated probability of X given the new information is closest to:
questions.explanation: B is correct. Applying Bayes' formula, Probability of X given the new information Probability of the new information given X/ Unconditional probability of the new information Prior probability of X, results in 0.5/0.6 x 0.7 = 0.5833~0.58. A is incorrect because Bayes' formula is misapplied as Probability of X given the new information = Probability of the new information given X/ Prior probability of X x Unconditional probability of the new information, 0.5/0.7 × 0.6 = 0.4286~0.43. C is incorrect because Bayes' formula is misapplied as Probability of X given the new information = Unconditional probability of the new information/Probability of the new information given X Prior probability of X, 0.6/0.5× 0.7 =0.84.

questions.question: Conflicts of interest between shareholders and management most likely occur when:

options.A: executive remuneration plans include deferred shares.
options.B: shareholders do not influence the design of executive compensation packages.
options.C: risky projects that match shareholders' risk tolerance are pursued by the company.

questions.explanation: B is correct. By allowing shareholders to express their views on remuneration matters, companies can limit the discretion of directors and managers in granting themselves excessive (or inadequate) remuneration, thus not allowing managers to have greater discretion over their own pay. Hence, if shareholders do not or miss to influence the design of executive compensation packages, conflicts of interest between shareholders and management may occur.
